title: Process/Thread Coordination Mechanisms in Parallel Computing
description: A flowchart illustrating the primary methods used to coordinate and synchronize processes and threads in a parallel computing environment to prevent conflicts and ensure correct execution.
relationships: Shared Memory enables communication via Locks/Mutexes & Semaphores
Message Passing enables communication via send/receive operations
Locks/Mutexes provide mutual exclusion for critical sections
Semaphores control access to a finite number of resources
Barriers synchronize process/thread progress at specific points
Condition Variables allow threads to wait for specific conditions
